questions.question: Which query retrieves all columns and all rows from the employees table?
Which query returns only the employees who work in the 'IT' department?

options.C: SELECT dept FROM employees WHERE name = 'IT';

questions.question: Which query counts how many employees are in the table?
Which query returns the total salary of all employees?
Which query finds the lowest salary in the employees table?
Which query returns the highest order amount from the orders table?
Which query counts the number of employees per department?

options.A: SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM employees ORDER BY dept;
options.B: SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY dept;
options.C: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP dept;
options.D: SELECT dept COUNT(*) FROM employees BY dept;

questions.question: Which query returns the total salary for each department?

options.A: SELECT dept, SUM(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY dept;
options.B: SELECT SUM(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY salary;
options.C: SELECT dept, TOTAL(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY dept;
options.D: SELECT dept, SUM(salary) FROM employees HAVING dept;

questions.question: Which query lists departments that have more than 1 employee?

options.A: SELECT dept FROM employees HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
options.B: SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE COUNT(*) > 1 GROUP BY dept;
options.C: SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY dept HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
options.D: SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY dept WHERE COUNT(*) > 1;

questions.question: Which statement correctly adds an email column (VARCHAR 100) to the employees table?

options.A: ALTER TABLE employees INSERT email VARCHAR(100);
options.B: UPDATE TABLE employees ADD email VARCHAR(100);
options.C: ALTER TABLE employees ADD email VARCHAR(100);
options.D: ALTER TABLE employees CREATE email VARCHAR(100);

questions.question: Which statement removes the salary column from the employees table?

options.A: ALTER TABLE employees DROP COLUMN salary;
options.B: ALTER TABLE employees DELETE COLUMN salary;

questions.question: Which statement renames the column name to full_name in the employees table?

options.A: ALTER TABLE employees CHANGE name TO full_name;
options.B: UPDATE TABLE employees RENAME name full_name;
options.C: ALTER TABLE employees MODIFY name = full_name;
options.D: ALTER TABLE employees RENAME COLUMN name TO full_name;

questions.question: Which statement renames the employees table to staff?

options.B: ALTER TABLE employees RENAME TO staff;

questions.question: Which query returns each employee name along with their department location, showing only employees that have a matching department?

options.A: SELECT e.name, d.location FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept = d.dept_name;
options.B: SELECT e.name, d.location FROM employees e, departments d;
options.C: SELECT e.name, d.location FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.dept = d.dept_name;
options.D: SELECT e.name, d.location FROM employees e OUTER JOIN departments d ON e.dept = d.dept_name;

questions.question: Which query returns all employees and their order amounts, including employees who have placed no orders (showing NULL for amount)?

options.A: SELECT e.name, o.amount FROM employees e INNER JOIN orders o ON e.name = o.customer;
options.B: SELECT e.name, o.amount FROM orders o LEFT JOIN employees e ON e.name = o.customer;
options.C: SELECT e.name, o.amount FROM employees e RIGHT JOIN orders o ON e.name = o.customer;
options.D: SELECT e.name, o.amount FROM employees e LEFT JOIN orders o ON e.name = o.customer;
